title: Sand mining feud: BJP neta burnt alive in car, 2 more killed in midnight ambush in Chhatisgarh
content: A violent sand mining turf war in Chhattisgarh's Koriya district resulted in a triple murder.  A BJP functionary was burnt alive in a luxury SUV, with two others dying from injuries after a suspected planned ambush. Police are investigating the escalating tensions between rival groups involved in the sand business.

title: The defection season: How rebels are redrawing India's political map
content: Indian opposition parties are experiencing internal turmoil and potential splits.  The Trinamool Congress, Shiv Sena (UBT), Samajwadi Party, and Aam Aadmi Party are all facing significant challenges.  These developments are reshaping the political landscape and impacting parliamentary arithmetic.  The trend suggests a season of political breakups is underway, with more expected.

title: Escape tunnel, CCTVs, 'modern gurukul': How Pune 'godman' exploited woman for 15 yrs
content: Pune police arrested a self-styled godman and seven associates for allegedly sexually assaulting, torturing, and financially exploiting a woman for over 15 years. Radhamohan Mishra, operating a 'modern gurukul,' allegedly used divine claims and psychological manipulation to control followers. The victim was isolated, surveilled, and subjected to severe abuse before escaping.

title: Sooryavanshi row: BCCI secretary breaks silence on Sri Lanka incident
content: BCCI secretary Devajit Saikia clarified the board's stance on the on-field altercation involving Vaibhav Sooryavanshi and Vishen Halambage. Saikia emphasised that disciplinary actions are solely the purview of match officials, not the BCCI. Sri Lanka Cricket has reportedly sanctioned Halambage for provocative remarks and Dickwella for excessive appealing following the Super Over match.

title: 'Telegram now a new dark web': Govt defends ban on app ahead of NEET retest
content: In an affidavit filed in response to Telegram's plea challenging the government's move to temporarily restrict access to the app ahead of the June 21 NEET-UG retest, the government countered that the "scale of the said channel itself demonstrates the capability of Telegram to facilitate mass dissemination of unlawful examination-related content". "Telegram has become the new dark web, linking threat actors.

title: Donald Trump makes 'Apple announcement' for Intel; says: Stupid Presidents took ...
content: Former President Donald Trump announced Apple will partner with Intel to design and manufacture chips in the US, aiming to revive the nation's semiconductor industry. This move, he stated, reverses years of manufacturing shifting overseas. The collaboration reportedly seeks to diversify Apple's supply chain beyond Taiwan, with Intel's shares seeing a significant surge following the news.

title: 'Op Tudwa' vs 'Op Tiger': Sanjay Raut claims 'rebel' MPs moved to Rajasthan
content: Shiv Sena (UBT) leader Sanjay Raut alleged dissident MPs received Rs 10 crore each and were moved to Rajasthan amid a party split. Six of nine Lok Sabha MPs skipped a crucial meeting, prompting Raut to initiate action for whip violation. Maharashtra minister Sanjay Shirsat, however, blamed Raut for the unrest and denied his party's involvement in 'Operation Tiger'.

title: NEET paper leak: Lessons from India's biggest exam scandal
content: In light of the recent NEET-UG paper leak, India's examination infrastructure is under fire, revealing significant systemic weaknesses. The fallout from the CBSE's grading mishaps and Bihar's logistical challenges sheds light on ongoing issues that disrupt the process for countless hopeful candidates. As these scandals emerge, questions regarding equity, responsibility, and confidence in the examination framework become increasingly critical.

title: Dell CEO Michael Dell makes one of largest public university donations in US history
content: Michael Dell has gifted a staggering $750 million to the University of Texas at Austin, propelling the creation of a groundbreaking AI-native hospital and research campus. This substantial donation, adding to his family's legacy of support, will also bolster scholarships, advanced computing, and vital medical research, underscoring Dell's enduring connection to his alma mater.

title: 'He will do whatever job you ask': Ex-England spinner's massive praise for India's unsung hero
content: Graeme Swann lauded Ishan Kishan's exceptional century, a blistering 125 off 79 balls, which was instrumental in India's commanding 170-run victory over Afghanistan. Kishan's remarkable innings, featuring a rapid transition from circumspect play to aggressive stroke-making, showcased his adaptability and improved batting prowess. This performance, alongside Shubman Gill's 154, propelled India to a massive total and secured the series.

title: Portugal needs to put 'GOAT' Ronaldo out to pasture
content: Is England actually good under a German? Will South Korea and Mexico bonhomie stop? What was the desi connection in Portugal vs Congo?

title: Google Gemini co-lead leaves for OpenAI less than 2 years after billion-dollar rehire
content: Noam Shazeer, Google's Gemini co-lead and self-described inventor of the LLM revolution, is leaving for OpenAI less than two years after Google paid a reported $2.7 billion to rehire him from Character. AI. The vice president of engineering called it a "difficult decision to move on." OpenAI CEO Sam Altman, who pursued Shazeer for a decade, hailed the hire as "worth the wait" as the AI talent war intensifies.

title: How Israel seized 1,000 sq km in two years, its biggest territorial expansion in decades
content: Israel has significantly expanded its military-held territory in Gaza, Lebanon, and Syria since October 2023, encompassing approximately 1,000 square kilometers. This move, described as creating "buffer zones," has displaced millions and raises concerns about permanence, despite not being recognized as new borders. This marks its biggest expansion of its militarily-held territories in years.

title: Next-gen battletanks: Europe reveals AI-powered armoured combat system; India accelerates FRCV
content: Europe is modernizing its heavy armour with KNDS's Capint tank, a hybrid design featuring an unmanned turret and advanced AI, set to replace aging French tanks. This move mirrors global trends towards digitised, autonomous warfare, with nations like the US, Russia, China, and India also developing next-generation combat vehicles. India's FRCV program emphasizes network-centric operations and human-machine teaming.

title: Harry Kane and Kate Goodland turned a childhood romance Into marriage
content: Harry Kane enters his third World Cup as England's captain and all-time top scorer, fresh off a 36-goal Bundesliga season with Bayern Munich. Away from the pitch, his wife Katie Goodland has been part of his story since childhood. The pair met at age four in Chingford, started dating as teenagers in 2011, and married in 2019. A qualified fitness coach with a Sports Science degree, Goodland now balances her own career with raising their four children while supporting Kane through England's latest push to end six decades of World Cup hurt.

title: Crissy Froyd claims Mike Silver liked old bikini photos after Russini Story Row
content: Crissy Froyd has accused The Athletic's Mike Silver of liking old bikini photos on her Instagram immediately after she published a column about the Mike Vrabel-Dianna Russini scandal, alleging the behaviour was targeted. Froyd, who was previously fired from USA Today Sports over her criticism of Russini, has alleged that at least six female reporters admitted to her that they traded intimacy for NFL access. Meanwhile, Russini has resigned from The Athletic, which is now conducting an internal investigation.

title: New claim about Jorge Messi’s health reframes Lionel Messi’s World Cup Tears
content: Lionel Messi revealed that his emotional reaction after Argentina’s World Cup win was unrelated to football. According to comments made by journalist Eduardo Feinmann on Radio Mitre, concerns about the health of Messi’s father, Jorge Messi, may have been behind the tears. Despite the personal situation, Messi starred in Argentina’s 3-0 victory over Algeria.

title: You're Watching Because of Him': Fans Slam Brooklyn Beckham's FIFA Ad
content: Brooklyn Beckham has sparked fresh debate after appearing in a new DoorDash advertisement linked to the FIFA World Cup 2026. The campaign has drawn attention because many viewers believe it makes light of his ongoing rift with his famous family. Brooklyn, the eldest son of David and Victoria Beckham, features in two promotional videos that hint at personal issues while encouraging fans to engage with the brand’s World Cup campaign.

title: NSE mega IPO: All you need to know about the Rs 30,000 crore offering
content: The National Stock Exchange of India has filed its draft red herring prospectus for a massive IPO, aiming to raise approximately Rs 30,000 crore. This offering is set to become India's largest corporate market debut, surpassing the Rs 27,859 crore IPO of Hyundai Motor India.

title: 'Forced me to drink urine, gave electric shocks': Pune woman alleges 15 years of abuse by 'guru'
content: Pune police arrested a self-styled guru and seven associates for allegedly sexually exploiting and cheating a woman. The guru, Radhamohan Mishra, reportedly subjected the victim to severe abuse for 15 years, including physical and sexual assault, and forced her to transfer property. Investigations revealed plans for an escape tunnel and recovered incriminating electronic devices and valuables.

title: Delhi doctor kills house help with cricket bat, stabs her in posh Mount Kailash
content: A 45-year-old domestic help was found brutally murdered on a building terrace in South Delhi's Mount Kailash area. Police arrested her employer, Dr. Manish Gupta, who allegedly bludgeoned and stabbed the victim. The motive remains unclear, with investigators exploring a potential personal dispute.

title: India-UK trade deal effective July 15: From cheaper cars to scotch, 7 key changes
content: India and the UK's Comprehensive Economic and Trade Agreement (CETA) takes effect July 15, promising expanded market access and reduced tariffs. The pact will boost Indian exports, offer tariff advantages, and liberalize trade in goods and services, including automotive and spirits. Professionals will benefit from social security exemptions, fostering deeper bilateral ties.

title: ‘Batwara 1947’ teaser out: Sunny Deol revisits the pain of partition
content: The teaser of ‘Batwara 1947’ has finally been unveiled, offering a glimpse into the emotional turmoil of India’s Partition. Led by Sunny Deol, the film explores courage and humanity amid chaos, while Preity Zinta appears briefly. Directed by Rajkumar Santoshi, the historical drama releases worldwide on August 14, 2026.

title: Justin Gaethje's 6-Month Suspension Explained After Beating Ilia Topuria
content: Justin Gaethje achieved one of the biggest victories of his UFC career. He defeated Ilia Topuria at UFC Freedom 250 on June 14. The event took place at the White House. Gaethje entered the fight as the underdog but produced an outstanding performance against the previously unbeaten champion. After four intense rounds, Topuria’s corner decided to stop the contest due to severe facial swelling and damage around his eyes.

title: India-Vietnam BrahMos deal in final stages, only minor clearances remain: BrahMos Aerospace chief
content: India is nearing a significant defence export deal with Vietnam for BrahMos supersonic cruise missiles, with final clearances pending. BrahMos Aerospace chief Jaiteerth Joshi revealed ongoing talks with other nations too. The program has seen substantial cost reductions and is developing advanced variants, enhancing India's defence export capabilities.

title: From rent to repairs: Why written rent agreement is essential for tenants
content: Securing your rental living with a written agreement is crucial, especially when verbal understandings have weaker legal backing. This document, executed on stamp paper, outlines rent, deposits, tenancy duration, and repair responsibilities. Essential documents like ownership proof and tenant police verification are required. Registration is mandatory for agreements over 11 months, ensuring legal clarity and protection for both parties.

title: Hajj pilgrim wins Rs 2.09 lakh compensation after travel firm fails to secure visa
content: A Srinagar consumer commission has ordered Albalaagh Tours and Travels to pay Rs 2.09 lakh to a pilgrim. The travel firm accepted advance payment for a Hajj package but failed to secure a visa, leading to the pilgrimage's cancellation. The commission found the company liable for unfair trade practices and deficient service, awarding refund, compensation, and litigation costs.

title: Guided mobile 120mm mortar system tested, brings precision smart shells to the fight
content: India's Nibe Limited has unveiled the Garudastra, a potent 120mm vehicle-mounted mortar system. This advanced weapon boasts rapid shoot-and-scoot capabilities, precision targeting with GPS and laser-guided munitions, and a high rate of fire. Designed for modern warfare, it offers enhanced survivability and effective engagement against fortified positions, aligning with India's self-reliance goals.

title: 'Spied with CCTVs, assaulted for talking to neighbours': Doctor husband held after newlywed's death
content: A 26-year-old newlywed woman in Ambernath died by suicide, with her family accusing her doctor husband and in-laws of dowry harassment and physical abuse. The deceased's family alleges constant surveillance via CCTV cameras and demands for more gifts. The husband, mother-in-law, and brother-in-law have been arrested following a police complaint.

title: 'Jumped to save mother': Indian teen dies in New York after falling from horse carriage
content: A tragic accident in New York's Central Park claimed the life of 18-year-old Indian student Romanch Mahajan. While on a horse-drawn carriage ride, the vehicle bolted, and Romanch heroically jumped to save his mother, suffering a fatal head injury. The incident has reignited calls to ban horse-drawn carriages in the park.

title: Captain Shubman Gill joins Kohli, Tendulkar in elite club after 154 vs Afghanistan
content: Shubman Gill's stellar 154 against Afghanistan in Lucknow secured a commanding 170-run victory for India, clinching the ODI series. This remarkable innings marked Gill as the seventh Indian captain to score over 150, joining legends like Tendulkar and Kohli. His performance underscores his growing stature in Indian cricket.

title: India eyes $2.5 billion loans from World Bank, ADB as subsidy costs rise
content: India is in discussions with the World Bank and Asian Development Bank for approximately $2.5 billion in loans to support urban infrastructure and job creation. This move aims to bolster capital for government spending, which has been impacted by rising oil prices and increased subsidy expenditures due to the Middle East conflict.

title: 'Not Bhagwant Mann': AAP cites '1,191 different angles' to defend Punjab CM in 'sacrilege' row
content: Punjab finance minister and AAP leader Harpal Singh Cheema asserted that a video allegedly showing chief minister Bhagwant Mann in a sacrilegious act is fake. Forensic reports from two independent laboratories confirm the individual in the video is not Mann, suggesting a defamation attempt by the opposition. This comes after the Akal Takht issued an edict against Mann over the clip.

title: Govt considers durability certificates for buildings after unsafe construction concerns
content: The Centre is considering a 'durability certificate' for buildings due to rising concerns over unsafe construction, following court-ordered demolitions of unfit structures. This initiative aims to improve construction quality and safety, especially after recent incidents like a building collapse in Delhi and a dilapidated structure's partial collapse in Varanasi.

title: Watch: Young fan teaches Messi a new celebration after historic WC hat-trick
content: Lionel Messi ignited the FIFA World Cup 2026 with a historic hat-trick against Algeria, becoming the oldest player to achieve the feat and equalling Miroslav Klose's record. A heartwarming viral video captured Messi learning a new goal celebration from a young fan, sparking anticipation for his potential record-breaking strike.

title: Sold cigarettes above printed price: Manufacturer, retailer fined Rs 10 lakh
content: A consumer commission in Aligarh has fined a cigarette manufacturer and a retailer Rs 10 lakh for overcharging a customer Rs 20 above the printed price. The commission ruled the seller acted as the manufacturer's agent, making both liable for unfair trade practices. The duo must also compensate the complainant.

title: Why BCCI is planning early IPL start from 2027? Saikia reveals the reasons
content: The BCCI is planning to kick off the IPL from March 10-May 15 starting in 2027, aiming to escape extreme weather. This shift seeks to mitigate disruptions from heatwaves and pre-monsoon rains affecting late-season matches. The tournament will maintain its 74-match format, with domestic schedules also being reviewed to align with the earlier start.

title: Andhra photographer’s global honour shines light on India’s documentary tradition
content: Australian Photographic Society awarded him for his series Risk as Job: The Daily Saga of Artisanal Coal Miners in May this year

title: TMC’s Aroop seeks freeze on party bank accounts amid leadership battle; fresh blow to Mamata camp
content: The development assumes significance as Mr. Biswas was removed as treasurer during a reorganisation by the Mamata Banerjee camp on June 5, with former MP Subhasish Chakraborty being named to the post

title: Veteran bureaucrat and TDB president Jayakumar returns to his original passion for painting
content: His 25th painting exhibition, Glimpses, at the Mauve Art Gallery on June 18, marks K. Jayakumar’s return to Thiruvananthapuram’s art scene after four years. The exhibition features 22 paintings, including 15 new works and a handful of older canvases selected from his earlier collections.

title: Explained | The ‘kafir screenshot’ case in Kerala
content: The screenshot came into the public domain on the eve of the 2024 general elections in the Vadakara constituency in Kozhikode

title: Revised social security pact with U.K. could save $500 million for Indian firms and workers
content: According to sources, India is also “happy that its concerns have been addressed” regarding the U.K.’s recent steel tariff announcements, which had temporarily halted the implementation of the bilateral trade deal.

title: PM Modi in Paris LIVE updates: Modi meets leading CEOs in France; discusses investments and expansion plans in India
content: The discussions with the CEOs of the French companies focused on strengthening cooperation across sectors ranging from shipping and logistics to railways, construction and artificial intelligence, according to the Ministry of External Affairs (MEA).

title: NTA urges NEET-UG candidates to stay calm, ignore rumours ahead of re-exam
content: National Testing Agency in a post on X warned against malpractice, saying, "Any attempt at malpractice will be dealt with firmly - because protecting honest, hardworking students is exactly what this is about."

title: Ram temple row: Why isn’t SP talking about Babri funds, asks U.P. Deputy CM
content: Brajesh Pathak said the people of the State know ‘very well’ that ‘these (SP) are the elements who fan riots and open fire on devotees of Ram’; he added that ‘the people of Uttar Pradesh will never forgive them’

title: Andhra Pradesh tops country in PM SHRI fund use, says Nara Lokesh
content: The Minister directed officials to build smart kitchens on the Kadapa model and to extend Talliki Vandanam benefits to orphaned children

title: Two-wheeler riders rewarded with 1 kg of tomato for wearing helmet at Pattukottai
content: Pattukottai traffic police give a pleasant surprise to law-abiding two-wheeler riders during the morning peak hour at the market

title: TVK-led government failed to secure Cauvery water for kuruvai cultivation: Edappadi Palaniswami
content: Mr. Palaniswami said although the government had announced 436 schemes across various departments at a Cabinet meeting, they did not find mention in the Governor’s address

title: Rupee rises 14 paise to close at 94.36 against USD
content: At the interbank foreign exchange, the rupee opened at 94.66 against the U.S. dollar and traded in the range of 94.18-94.71. Eventually, it settled at 94.36 (provisional), up 14 paise from its previous close

title: War heroes, national icons to replace British-era names on 21 roads in Secunderabad Cantonment
content: Bolton Road will become Atal Bihari Vajpayee Road, Noble Road will be renamed A.P.J. Abdul Kalam Road and Lytton Road will bear the name of Netaji Subash Chandra Bose

title: Chittoor Collector says steps on to ensure remunerative prices for Totapuri mango farmers
content: Totapuri prices have fallen to ₹5 to ₹6 a kg on weak demand, the Collector said, citing surplus pulp stocks with Coca-Cola and Pepsi

title: Amit Shah, U.S. Ambassador Gor discuss enhancing India-U.S. security cooperation
content: Commenting on the meeting with Mr. Shah, Mr. Gor said that, they discussed on “collaboration to combat terrorism, shield our people from narcotics and illicit drugs, secure our borders, and jointly bring criminals to justice in both nations.”

title: Need to protect forests across country: SC
content: The court observed that some of the States in the country have natural paradises like forests, including Jharkhand, and they need to be protected

title: Delhi HC reserves judgment on Telegram’s plea challenging temporary ban ahead of NEET retest
content: Centre flagged security risks and lack of proactive measure against “illegal, suspicious” channels; submitted list of action taken against the platform by other countries

title: Madras High Court refuses to grant relief to MBBS student whose college fees was seized by NIA
content: Chief Justice’s Bench says the medical student cannot assert an equitable right to benefit from the fruits of a crime

title: Host biology drives malaria treatment failures, reveals RGCB study
content: The study suggests that targeting host-parasite interactions could improve treatment efficacy and reduce relapses, particularly in vulnerable groups like children and anaemic patients.

title: Former MD of Anil Ambani group Sateesh Seth sent to 14-day judicial custody in PMLA case
content: Court lets Sateesh Seth to carry his glasses and medicines while in jail and directs jail authorities to take an appropriate decision regarding his request for a bed in accordance with the jail manual.

title: Security forces will retaliate in self-defence against armed people: Manipur DGP
content: The call for strict action follows CRPF Director-General G.P. Singh’s advice to security personnel to neutralise miscreants moving in civilian areas with weapons

title: Manipur government will not remain mute spectator to atrocities: CM Khemchand on ‘killing’ of Naga hostages
content: The United Naga Council has imposed a 24-hour shutdown in parts of Manipur from 6 a.m. on June 11 to protest against the killing of Naga hostages

title: Arrests, mass resignations, ‘bulldozer action’ deepen Trinamool’s crisis across West Bengal
content: Drives against illegal construction, notices to properties linked to senior Trinamool leaders, and mass resignations in local bodies pile pressure on the TMC three weeks after the BJP assumed power in West Bengal

title: Internal bickering, resignations and public anger, Trinamool battling on multiple fronts
content: The differences between two senior MPs of the party, Kakoli Ghosh Dastidar and Kalyan Banerjee, became public. Trinamool MP Sougata Roy faced protests, and eggs were hurled at his car

title: PIL challenging free travel for women in KSRTC ordinary buses: High Court reserves matter for orders
content: The petitioner contended that no study was conducted before rolling out the scheme that is aimed at making available free travel for women

title: Certain elements want to keep Manipur burning: CM Khemchand Singh
content: Addressing a programme in his Assembly constituency in Imphal West district, he says the continuing violence has affected developmental activities in the Northeast State

title: 20,876 kg of ganja worth ₹20 crore destroyed in Anakapalli; ASR cultivation wiped out, says IG
content: Officials said ASR district’s ganja cultivation, 7,515 acres in 2021-22, had been wiped out by January 2026 through drone surveillance and alternative crops

title: Governor’s address: T.N. govt. vows to oppose new Mullaperiyar dam proposal, take steps to halt Mekedatu
content: The Governor, in his address to the House, also said that to improve the administration of the State’s historic spiritual temples, the government will carry out structural reforms in the HR&CE Department

title: T.N. Assembly to pass resolution demanding State’s due share of financial devolution: Governor Arlekar
content: Governor Arlekar announced a resolution seeking Tamil Nadu’s share of Union taxes and the formation of a legal committee to pursue the issue in the Supreme Court

title: ‘Kafir screenshot’ case: Vadakara court grants police two-day custody of DYFI leader Jithin Bhaskar
content: The prosecution submitted that custodial interrogation was necessary to ascertain whether Mr. Bhaskar had used a computer or any other digital device to create the disputed screenshot and to recover electronic evidence relevant to the investigation.

title: Pachaiyappa’s College to admit women students at undergraduate level
content: “As the first woman principal of this legacy institution, I feel privileged to be part of history,” principal Baby Gulnaz said

title: West Bengal moves Supreme Court against early release of 1993 Bowbazar blast convict
content: In a judgment delivered on June 5, the Delhi High Court directed Rashid Khan’s premature release, holding that the period of incarceration already undergone was sufficient to meet the objectives of punishment and deterrence.

title: Watch: The multi cornered fight for Punjab | Talking Politics
content: Punjab goes to polls next year along with a few other States. Suddenly, all parties in the fray have become very active in the political field. What’s the difference between the scene in 2022 and now? Has the novelty of the ruling Aam Aadmi Party faded? We are joined by The Hindu’s Punjab correspondent, Vikas Vasudeva.

title: ED arrests former AP Minister’s son in liquor transport scam probe
content: The Central agency is investigating alleged irregularities in liquor transportation contracts awarded by the Andhra Pradesh State Beverages Corporation Limited.

title: Escalating cost of fresh fish upends ordinary household budgets
content: With the seasonal trawling ban in place, most of the daily catch comes from traditional fishing boats, including plank-built catamarans and dinghies fashioned from discarded styrofoam containers, which venture out to sea at dusk and return by dawn

title: ‘Kafir’ screenshot case: court extends deadline to submit report
content: The case involves the alleged circulation of a fake screenshot during the Vadakara Lok Sabha election labelling Left Democratic Front candidate K.K. Shailaja as a ‘kafir’ to incite communal tension

title: ‘Kafir screenshot’ row: SIT zeroing in on more suspects through new WhatsApp groups
content: Investigators are now focussing on the Kadathanadu Sakhakkal and Bavuppara Sakhakkal WhatsApp groups in Kozhikode district with plans to interrogate some of the already identified group admins

title: T.N. Governor’s address: TVK govt. to establish Investor Promotion Commission to fast-track approvals for companies
content: In his address, the Governor said the government is firm that industries must grow and workers must thrive in order to achieve the target set by Chief Minister C. Joseph Vijay of transforming Tamil Nadu into a $1.5-trillion economy by 2036

title: Banning Telegram will not stop paper leaks: Kejriwal
content: Arvind Kejriwal asserted that banning the messaging platform was not the solution to the problem and claimed that the authorities were not serious about tackling the issue

title: Watch: Telegram under fire: NTA’s crackdown explained | Above the Fold | 17.06.2026
content: Telegram challenges its temporary NEET block, fresh details emerge on the U.S.-Iran ceasefire deal, and Messi makes World Cup history with a hat-trick - Top stories of the day.

title: Tamil Nadu Assured Pension Scheme: Government frames rules, procedures
content: The rules provide for a two-stage exit option from the TAPS framework in case eligible government staff wish to exit TAPS

title: Messi event row: Aroop Biswas appears after skipping three police summons
content: Former West Bengal sports Minister Aroop Biswas arrived at the Bidhannagar Police Commissionerate headquarters around 9.55 a.m., after skipping three previous summonses

title: Sea Cadet Corps invites applications for 2026 batch
content: Children aged 10 to 12, studying in Classes V to VII, can collect forms at the INS Circars swimming pool on June 20

title: My son will not hold any position in AIADMK, says Edappadi K. Palaniswami
content: “He will remain only a primary member of the party and will not occupy any post in the organisation,” he told journalists after the Governor’s address to the Assembly

title: T.N. CM Vijay announces Kuruvai Special Package for paddy cultivation at a cost of ₹134.83 crore
content: Due to the delay in southwest monsoon, the Mettur dam was not opened on the customary date of June 12; a release states the CM has announced the Kuruvai Special Package to protect farmers’ livelihoods and encourage paddy cultivation

title: Mettur Dam not to be opened for delta irrigation on June 12
content: The government will decide on the release of water for irrigation if the region receives heavy rainfall in the coming days

title: Air Force ferrying NEET question papers by aircraft
content: Bundles flown to more than 20 locations across the country; papers delivered to several northern States, while dry runs were conducted at selected locations across southern India

title: Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan holds  review meeting for NEET re-examination preparations
content: Emphasising on the importance of coordination at all levels, the Minister informed that officers designated by the Ministry will reach all the States to coordinate the activities pertaining to the re-examination process and will report to the Command Centre headed by the DG, NTA

title: Bihar Horror: Woman Gang-Raped, Bullet Inserted In Private Part, Cuts On Body
content: The survivor said the attackers took her to a desolate place outside her house and raped her. When she tried to raise an alarm, they sliced her chest and thigh with a blade.

title: Sanjay Raut's "Traitor" Comment Upset Team Uddhav Rebels Again, Say Sources
content: There has been no official response from Nagesh Aashtikar, Sanjay Deshmukh, Sanjay Jadhav, Sanjay Dina Patil, Omprakash Rajenimbalkar and Bhausaheb Wakchaure, who had skipped the meeting.

title: Rare, Under-Studied Goat-Antelope Spotted In Sikkim After Nearly 3 Decades
content: Mishmi Takin: During a routine patrolling exercise conducted in Bakuchen under Tingda Reserve Forest, forest officials recorded the first-ever video footage of the species from the area.

title: Telegram Is "Like Frankenstein": Centre Defends Move To Block Platform
content: "Telegram has become the new dark web... Criminals have rapidly adopted Telegram to post links on channels that connect to dark web forums," the Centre told the HIgh Court today

title: After PM Modi-Trump G7 Meet, Amit Shah Holds Security Talks With US Envoy
content: He said under the leadership of Prime Minister Modi, India is firmly committed to advancing the India-U.S. Comprehensive Global Strategic Partnership and ensuring that the people of both nations benefit from the bilateral relationship.

title: French Tanker Passes Strait Of Hormuz After War Deal: Report
content: A French-flagged LNG tanker left the Gulf through the Strait of Hormuz on Thursday, a tracking platform said, as an agreement to end the US-Iran war paved the way for the key passage's reopening.

title: Assam Prepares For Ambubachi Mela, Over 8 Lakh Devotees Expected
content: The festival will commence with Pravritti on June 22 and conclude with Nivritti on June 26, when the temple doors reopen after remaining closed for three days.

title: Video: Ukrainian Drone Hits Russian Refinery, Blows Tank Lid Off
content: According to Russian officials, Ukraine launched one of its biggest drone attacks on Moscow in more than two years.

title: Ex-Agniveers To Get 20% Reservation In Delhi Government Jobs
content: Departments have also been authorised to deploy former Agniveers in roles aligned with the skills, discipline and training acquired during their service.

title: Pune 'Godman' Abused Woman For Years, Made Her Drink His Urine
content: Radheshyam Mishra aka Radhamohan Mishra ran what he called a 'modern gurukul' in the Ubale Nagar area of Wagholi in Pune.

title: Opinion: Social Media Monopoly Looked Unbreakable For A Decade. It Doesn't Anymore
content: The real question about X was never about the platform. It was always about the architecture Elon Musk envisioned around it.

title: "Position Remains Unchanged": Tamil Nadu vs Kerala Again Over Mullaperiyar Dam
content: In a statement, the Tamil Nadu government said that any move by Kerala to push for a new dam would be opposed.

title: Jobs, Free Bus Travel, Rs 2,000 Crore Roads: Karnataka's Big Welfare Push
content: According to government sources, the administration sees Karnataka's youth population as a critical driver of growth and has sought to place education and employment at the centre of its governance agenda.

title: UAE Announces Social Media Ban For Children Under 15
content: The United Arab Emirates announced a social media ban for children under 15 on Thursday, joining a growing group of countries including Australia, Britain and Canada to take similar measures.

title: Why India's New Data Law Is A Costly Wake-Up Call
content: DPDP Act: A startup spending Rs 1-2 crore annually could see operational costs rise by an additional Rs 20-40 lakh, as per experts.

title: Powerful El Nino To Impact Global Weather Through 2027, Scientists Warn
content: Scientists classify a strong El Nino when temperatures in the Nino 3.4 region rise between 1.5C and 1.9C above average, while anomalies above 2C are associated with very strong events.

title: Who Was Joshua Baer, Capital Factory CEO Killed In Texas Plane Crash
content: Baer was traveling in a business jet with five other people when it crashed onto Loop 20, a major highway in Laredo and caught fire.

content: the fossils date back to the miocene epoch, a period known for significant biodiversity and climatic changes . it was unearthed from the Baripada Fossil Bed and identified by Dr Debabrata Nandi .
